transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: Thanks for making the time. To start, tell me about your work.

transcript.speaker: P9
transcript.text: I'm a admin on a enterprise team.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: Walk me through getting your team set up.

transcript.speaker: P9
transcript.text: Again it was the permissions handoff — we couldn't tell who had the rights to grant access.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: How did pricing factor into your decision?

transcript.speaker: P9
transcript.text: The pricing was opaque — I genuinely could not forecast the cost for next quarter.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: How do you share results with stakeholders?

transcript.speaker: P9
transcript.text: Reporting out is manual — I rebuild the same deck for stakeholders each week.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: That’s really helpful — anything else before we wrap?

transcript.speaker: P9
transcript.text: No, I think that covers the main pain points.

themes.label: Onboarding stalls at the permissions handoff
themes.summary: New teams stall at the handoff between inviting teammates and granting the data access that an admin has to approve.

themes.label: Pricing opacity erodes trust before the first invoice
themes.summary: Buyers cannot predict what the product will cost next quarter, so they stop advocating for it internally with their manager and finance.

themes.label: Reporting to stakeholders is manual
themes.summary: Sharing findings with stakeholders means exporting dashboards into slides and pdf by hand every week.
